steps.title: Rewrite to match the given root form
steps.content: Start with $$x^2+9x+11=0.$$ Multiply by 4 so the square will have integer coefficients: $$4x^2+36x+44=0.$$

steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Move the constant and complete the square: $$4x^2+36x=-44$$ $$(2x+9)^2=37.$$ Therefore $$2x+9=\pm\sqrt{37},$$ so $$x=\frac{-9\pm\sqrt{37}}{2}.$$ In the given form, $k=37$.

The expression under the radical is the requested $k$, so once the completed-square form has $\sqrt{37}$, the value of $k$ is fixed.
